title: Myosotis lutea
taxonomy: Myosotis lutea is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae. It belongs to the genus Myosotis, which includes approximately 60 species of forget-me-nots. The plant is native to Europe and Western Asia. Myosotis lutea is also known as golden forget-me-not due to its bright yellow flowers. Its classification is further divided into the kingdom Plantae and the class Magnoliopsida.

intro: Myosotis lutea, commonly known as yellow forget-me-not, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Boraginaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Myosotis lutea include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects.
